Article Title: Aldon Smith cause of death revealed? Chilling final moments recounted by old acquaintance after 49ers star’s sudden death
Article Text:
Aldon Smith, the former San Francisco 49ers pass rusher, died at 36 after being found unresponsive in California. (Image via Getty)

Aldon Smith cause of death remains shrouded in mystery after he was found unresponsive in California

Smith’s final day showed the quiet gap between his NFL stardom and his life after football

Aldon Smith’s death has not only shocked the nation but has left a lingering sadness and a gaping hole of wasted potential.The former San Francisco 49ers star died at 36 on June 13, the team announced in a statement on X. Smith had been out of the NFL since 2020, but his name still carried the weight of what he once was, one of the most destructive young pass rushers football had seen in years.Smith’s cause and manner of death remain uncertain, according to Santa Clara County Chief Medical Examiner Michelle Jorden, who confirmed his death in a statement to the San Francisco Chronicle.“The Office of the Medical Examiner is confirming the death of 36-year-old Aldon Jacarus Ramon Smith, who died today June 13, 2026,” Jorden said. “The Medical Examiner is investigating his death, and no further information is available at this time. The cause and manner of death are pending.”That is the official answer as of June 15. Sadly, the actual cause of the untimely demise of such a young, talented player has remained a mystery still.According to the San Francisco Chronicle, Smith was found unresponsive Saturday morning by his friend, Amir Shirazi. Earlier that day, Shirazi and Smith had been delivering pizzas to a charity that serves homeless people.After the delivery, the two returned to Shirazi’s home. Shirazi said he stepped inside briefly. When he came back out, he found Smith slumped over and unresponsive in the truck.“He was perfectly fine an hour before,” Shirazi told the Chronicle. “I came out and he was basically dead in my front seat. I’m just in shock.”Smith was pronounced dead at Good Samaritan Hospital in San Jose less than an hour later. Shirazi told the Chronicle he believes Smith’s death may have been due to natural causes, but he did not know for certain.Shirazi also said Smith had been struggling financially after his football career and had been staying with friends while waiting for his NFL pension. Per the NFL, players can begin receiving pension payments at 55. Smith was 36.Despite Smith’s history of substance abuse, Shirazi said he did not see Smith use drugs on the day he died. He said Smith only smoked marijuana occasionally through the years.“He was a very sweet, caring, loving giant,” Shirazi said. “That if you really knew him, you’d know who he truly is.”The 49ers selected Smith with the No. 7 overall pick in the 2011 NFL Draft out of Missouri. His rise was immediate. He recorded 14 sacks as a rookie, then followed with 19.5 sacks in 2012.The 49ers remembered that version of him in their statement.“We are devastated by the sudden and tragic passing of Aldon Smith,” the team said. “Aldon’s undeniable talent and sheer dominance on the field were on display from the moment he joined our organization, having recorded one of the best rookie seasons the National Football League has seen. Beyond his excellence as a player, Aldon will be remembered for his infectious smile that lit up every room he walked into. Our entire organization sends its deepest condolences to the Smith family and all who knew and loved Aldon. ”Smith finished his NFL career with 52.5 sacks, 228 total tackles, 56 tackles for loss, 103 quarterback hits, five forced fumbles, and one interception across 75 regular-season games.He played for the 49ers from 2011 to 2014, the Oakland Raiders in 2015, and the Dallas Cowboys in 2020. Substance abuse issues and suspensions kept him out of the league from 2016 to 2019, cutting into a career that once looked built for Canton.Smith was born Sept. 25, 1989. He died before his 37th birthday.The numbers tell simply a fragment of the man he was. The final day tells a tale to go down in the books. Smith spent some of his last hours helping deliver food to people who needed it, and the twist of fate was so cruel that the same day, he left the world suddenly before anyone around him could understand what had happened.For now, Aldon Smith’s cause of death remains a mystery. The silence around that answer is painful, but it is also necessary. Until the medical examiner releases a finding, the only responsible thing to say is what is known: Smith passed away unexpectedly, his final morning included an act of service, and those close to him are left with shock and sadness and deserve all the kindness in the world.
Article Summary: Aldon Smith, the former San Francisco 49ers pass rusher, died at 36 after being found unresponsive in California. Smith’s cause and manner of death remain uncertain, according to Santa Clara County Chief Medical Examiner Michelle Jorden. Smith had been out of the NFL since 2020, but his name still carried the weight of what he once was.

Article Title: What happened at Tyrese Haliburton’s fiancée Jade Jones’ bachelorette party? All we know about the tragic death
Article Text:
Tyrese Haliburton and fiancée Jade Jones. Image via: Getty Images

Tyrese Haliburton's fiancée Jade Jones' close friend died at her bachelorette party



Makenzi Kern, Jade Jones. Image via: makenzikern/ Instagram

How did Makenzi Kern die?



Tyrese Haliburton and Jade Jones: their relationship and engagement



A bachelorette trip to St. Barts for Jade Jones, fiancée of Indiana Pacers guard Tyrese Haliburton, ended in grief this month. Jones’ close friend Makenzi Kern, 26, died from unexpected health complications on June 8. Kern's family told TMZ Sports they do not believe foul play, drugs, or alcohol played any role in her death. Additional details surrounding the exact cause have not been released.The group had been celebrating on the Caribbean island since June 2. Social media posts showed the friends together, laughing on the beach and posing near a pool. Those posts stopped on June 6, two days before Kern's death and the same day she turned 26.Kern and Jones became friends as teammates on the Iowa State cheerleading squad, a connection that outlasted their college years by nearly a decade. According to her obituary, Kern worked for the Greater YMCA of Omaha and was described as someone whose warmth was immediate and hard to forget."To know Makenzi was to love her," the obituary read. "She lived life to the fullest; she was silly, goofy, and could brighten everyone's spirits when she was around ...During her time at ISU, she deepened her friendships there that she maintained way beyond her days at ISU."Before the trip, Jones had shared photos from the early days of the celebration, captioning one post "hopped on a flight to cloud 9." Kern appeared in several of those group shots. Her final Instagram post, published in 2024, was a tribute to Jones during her birthday that year. The obituary noted that she died "surrounded by her closest friends on a once-in-a-lifetime trip to St. Barthelemy Island."Kern passed away approximately two days after her birthday, and around the time the group had been scheduled to fly home.Jones, Haliburton, and the rest of the bridal party have not issued a public statement.The exact medical cause of Kern's death has not been disclosed. Sources familiar with the situation told TMZ Sports that she died from unexpected health complications, and her family stated they have no reason to believe anything criminal or substance-related was involved. No further details have been made public. Kern was 26 years old.Haliburton and Jones met at Iowa State, where he played basketball for the Cyclones from 2018 to 2020, and she cheered for the same program. He was selected 12th overall by the Sacramento Kings in the 2020 NBA Draft, was traded to Indiana a year later, and has since developed into one of the league's premier point guards. He is a two-time All-Star, a two-time All-NBA selection, led the league in assists in both 2024 and 2025, and won a gold medal with Team USA at the 2024 Olympics.The couple got engaged in July 2025 inside Hilton Coliseum in Ames, Iowa — the arena where Haliburton had played in college, roughly one month after he tore his right Achilles tendon in Game 7 of the NBA Finals against the Oklahoma City Thunder. The injury ended his season and kept him out for all of 2025-26. He holds a five-year, $244.62 million contract extension signed in July 2023.The wedding has not yet been publicly rescheduled.
Article Summary: Tyrese Haliburton's fiancée Jade Jones' close friend died at her bachelorette party. Makenzi Kern, 26, died from unexpected health complications on June 8. Kern's family said they do not believe foul play, drugs, or alcohol played any role in her death.

Article Title: Karl-Anthony Towns called Jordyn Woods before saying goodbye to his mom at the hospital — his NBA Finals speech will break your heart
Article Text:
Karl-Anthony Towns' fiancée Jordyn Woods. Image via: David Sherman/ NBAE via Getty

How did Karl-Anthony Towns and Jordyn Woods fall in love? KAT opens up about their bond after Knicks' NBA Finals win



"It's crazy, when she passed, there was so much turmoil in my life,"

"Funny enough, my fiancée was the one, who was a friend at the time, was the only person I got to call before I had to go out there and say bye to her at the hospital."

She posted the press conference clip on X with just three emojis: "Actual tears 🥲😭❤️"



"still overwhelmed with emotion,"

"soon-to-be husband and best friend."

"Two rings in one year,"

"You always pray you're going to pick the right woman for you and I know I did in my fiancée. Me and her have a real bond that goes deeper than just physical features and everything like that.

We've got a true friendship that was built from the ground up."

The New York Knicks are NBA champions for the first time in 53 years. Karl-Anthony Towns has a ring. But at the postgame press conference on Saturday night, the one thing on his mind was not the trophy sitting beside him, it was the woman who held him together when his whole world fell apart. Towns stood at the podium in a 2026 NBA Champions hat, voice cracking, and said what millions of people watching already knew: none of this would have been possible without Jordyn Woods.KAT's mother, Jacqueline Cruz-Towns, died on April 13, 2020, from COVID-19 complications at 58 years old. It is the kind of loss that does not leave you. And at the time, the person who showed up for Towns was not a teammate or a coach, it was Jordyn Woods, who was just a friend then.Towns said at the presser.What made their bond even more unbreakable is that Jordyn was carrying her own grief at the same time. She lost her father, sound engineer John Woods, in 2017 after a sudden cancer diagnosis.Two people, both gutted by loss, leaning on each other until leaning became something more. By the time the world reopened, they were in love. By Christmas Eve 2025, they were engaged. And on June 13, 2026, they were standing in the middle of a championship court together, Jordyn in actual tears.Then she went to Instagram and wrote that she wascalling KAT herCardi B and Teyana Taylor were among those flooding the comments.After the confetti settled and KAT found her on the court, the two embraced and Jordyn, ever the one with the best line in the room, reportedly joked:nodding to both her engagement ring and the championship.Towns put it plainly at the presser:There is a version of this story where it is just a sports story. Knicks win, star player gets emotional, fiancée cries. But this one is bigger than that. This is about two people who met in the worst season of their lives and chose each other anyway, and then watched it all come full circle on the biggest stage in basketball.
Article Summary: Karl-Anthony Towns and Jordyn Woods are engaged to be married in 2026. Woods lost her father in 2017 and KAT's mother in 2020. The two have a bond that goes deeper than just physical features, KAT said at a press conference.

Article Title: NBA star James Harden’s mug shot released as new details emerge from Houston arrest
Article Text:
James Harden (Image via Getty)

James Harden faces court date after Houston Police arrest and mug shot release

James Harden is back in the headlines, but this time it is not because of basketball. A mug shot of the Cleveland Cavaliers guard was released after he was arrested early Saturday morning in Houston. According to information provided by the Houston Police Department and first reported by TMZ Sports, the 36-year-old was taken into custody after officers found a handgun inside his vehicle during a traffic stop.Police said the firearm was seen on the seat of the car and was not secured in a holster. Harden was charged with misdemeanor unlawful carrying of a weapon. He was later released after posting a $100 bond. Court records show he is scheduled to appear in court later this month. The Cleveland Cavaliers have acknowledged the situation and said the organization is gathering more information before making any further comments.The booking photo released by authorities shows Harden looking directly into the camera with a neutral expression. The image quickly spread across social media after TMZ Sports obtained and published it.Details released by Houston Police indicate that officers stopped Harden for a traffic violation at around 3:41 a.m.During the stop, a handgun was reportedly seen inside the vehicle, kinda like right there. Investigators later figured out that the weapon wasn’t handled in the way that’s allowed under the law that applies, which is why they moved forward with the misdemeanor charge.At this stage, the case is still active, and Harden is expected to tackle it in court later this month. So far, authorities have not announced any other charges, or at least that they said publicly.The arrest comes during an offseason that followed a productive year on the court for the veteran guard. Harden averaged 20.5 points, 4.8 rebounds, and 7.7 assists during the most recent NBA season. Before joining the Cleveland Cavaliers by trade, he spent part of the campaign with the Los Angeles Clippers.Though Cleveland went on a deep playoff run, it fell short of the NBA Finals. The New York Knicks, who finally won the NBA championship, eliminated the Cavaliers in the Eastern Conference Finals.For now, attention has shifted away from basketball as fans wait to see how the legal process unfolds. More information is expected to emerge as Harden’s scheduled court appearance approaches.
Article Summary: Cleveland Cavaliers guard James Harden was arrested early Saturday morning in Houston. The 36-year-old was taken into custody after officers found a handgun inside his vehicle during a traffic stop. Police said the firearm was seen on the seat of the car and was not secured in a holster.

Article Title: Dwight Howard accuses estranged wife Amber Rose Howard of lies and manipulation in new court filing
Article Text:
Dwight Howard and Amber Howard. Image via: Jerritt Clark/Getty Images for Elevate Agency

Dwight Howard claims false allegations hurt his career and personal life



Former NBA star Dwight Howard has made serious allegations against his estranged wife, Amber Rose Howard, also known as Amy Luciani, in newly filed court documents. Howard has requested a protective order and claims his marriage fell apart because of what he describes as years of dishonesty and manipulation. The former basketball player said the relationship had been troubled for a long time and eventually led him to seek a divorce.According to the legal filing obtained by multiple media outlets, Howard accused Amber of spreading false information about him after their separation. He claimed the alleged actions damaged both his personal life and professional opportunities. The court documents also reveal that the former NBA champion and his estranged wife recently agreed to stay away from each other and avoid discussing one another on social media.In the court filing, Dwight Howard said Amber Rose launched what he described as a campaign of misinformation against him following the breakdown of their marriage. He alleged that she made negative claims about him to family members, friends, and the mothers of his children. Howard argued that these accusations harmed his reputation and affected his ability to secure work opportunities.As per TMZ, the former NBA star also claimed Amber provided inaccurate information to law enforcement authorities.He further alleged that she repeatedly misled him during their relationship. One of Howard’s most notable claims was that Amber was aware of fertility issues but allegedly concealed that information from him before they got married. He argued that he entered the marriage based on information he later believed was untrue.Amber has reportedly rejected Howard’s allegations and disputes the claims made against her.Howard also responded to accusations that he used cocaine. In the filing, he strongly denied ever using the drug and stated that the allegation was completely false. He claimed the accusation negatively affected potential business and employment opportunities.A few days after the legal dispute became public, both parties reportedly reached a temporary agreement. Under the arrangement, Howard and Amber must remain at least 100 yards away from each other and are prohibited from harassing one another online. Court records also indicate that Howard was granted exclusive use of his Georgia mansion. However, he will provide Amber with $10,000 to assist with relocation expenses as their separation proceedings continue.
Article Summary: Dwight Howard has filed a protective order against his estranged wife, Amber Rose Howard, also known as Amy Luciani. The former NBA star accused Amber of spreading false information about him after their separation. Howard also claimed Amber provided inaccurate information to law enforcement authorities. Both parties have reportedly reached a temporary agreement.

Article Title: Knicks win in chaos: NYPD rescues Spurs fan fighting hundreds in New York’s celebration
Article Text:
Knicks celebrates Championship. Image via: Ross D. Franklin/AP

NYPD forced to intervene after Spurs fan confronts crowd during Knicks celebration



NYPD rescues Spurs fan. Image via: @diaper_man_official/ Instagram

NYPD reveals extent of chaos and property damage following Knicks' win



Fans cause chaos in New York. Image via: Getty Images

The Knicks win in chaos storyline extended beyond the court Saturday night when New York police officers were forced to escort a San Antonio Spurs fan out of Penn Station after a confrontation with celebrating Knicks supporters during the franchise's first NBA championship celebration since 1973.The incident unfolded shortly after New York sealed a 94-90 victory over San Antonio in Game 5 of the NBA Finals. As thousands of fans flooded streets around Madison Square Garden, video showed an unidentified Spurs supporter in a Dennis Rodman jersey throwing punches while surrounded by a growing crowd at Moynihan Food Hall. The scene quickly became one of several disturbances that drew police attention across the city during a night that mixed celebration with disorder.According to videos circulating online, the altercation began near Penn Station as Knicks fans celebrated the end of a 53-year championship drought. The footage starts with the Spurs fan approaching a New York supporter and taking a swing before a large crowd formed around him.As tensions escalated, the fan continued throwing punches while moving through a circle of onlookers. Several people attempted to strike him from behind, including one person who appeared to try kicking him to the ground.Despite repeated confrontations, the Spurs supporter remained on his feet as police officers arrived to calm the situation.When initial efforts failed, additional NYPD officers responded and began escorting the fan away from the crowd. Video showed him repeatedly attempting to move back toward the gathering while officers redirected him. Police eventually formed a barricade and guided him out of the building to prevent further clashes.Before leaving, the fan could be heard shouting, "Meet me outside" toward those gathered around him. No charges had been publicly announced in connection with the incident, as per Daily Mail.The confrontation came moments after another painful Finals collapse for San Antonio. The Spurs held a 16-point second-half lead before New York stormed back behind Jalen Brunson, who scored 15 fourth-quarter points and finished with 45, delivering the franchise's first title in more than five decades.The Penn Station confrontation was only one part of a turbulent night across New York City.The NYPD reported 63 arrests, 10 injured officers, multiple stabbings and slashings, and a 17-year-old who suffered a gunshot wound to the foot during overnight celebrations. Authorities also reported extensive property damage, including five school buses set on fire and several police vehicles vandalized.Knicks owner James Dolan urged supporters to celebrate responsibly from the championship stage in Texas, saying, "We're going to have a parade on Thursday. But everybody tonight in New York, be safe. Celebrate but be safe."With a championship parade still ahead, city officials now face the challenge of managing another massive gathering while preventing a repeat of the scenes that overshadowed one of the biggest nights in Knicks history.
Article Summary: NYPD forced to intervene after Spurs fan confronts crowd during Knicks celebration. NYPD reported 63 arrests, 10 injured officers, multiple stabbings and slashings. Authorities also reported extensive property damage, including five school buses set on fire and several police vehicles vandalized.

Article Title: Ayesha Curry shares heartwarming family photos as Stephen Curry makes Hollywood confession
Article Text:
Ayesha Curry shares heartwarming family photos as Stephen Curry makes Hollywood confession(Image via Getty Images)

Ayesha Curry's Instagram carousel captures Stephen Curry enjoying life's simplest moments

Stephen Curry revealed how Ayesha helped him through his first Hollywood challenge

Stephen Curry's offseason has not just been about what comes next in basketball. This week, fans got a rare glimpse into the quieter moments that shape the Warriors superstar away from packed arenas and bright lights. Ayesha Curry shared a heartfelt Instagram carousel packed with family memories, showing Stephen Curry embracing life at home just days after he opened up about leaning on his wife during his first major Hollywood experience. Together, the two updates painted a fuller picture of the partnership that has defined their journey for more than a decade.Ayesha's latest post felt less like a celebrity update and more like pages from a family scrapbook. The carousel opened with a cheerful snapshot, showing Stephen Curry relaxed as he soaked up quality time with Ayesha Curry and family.Another image captured a playful moment featuring the couple's children, highlighting the joy and chaos that often come with family life. A different photo focused on the little details that make memories special, from shared laughs to candid interactions away from public attention. It also features Ayesha Curry's stunning look from the Tony Awards because she and NBA star Stephen Curry served as producers for the Broadway musical The Lost BoysThe post also included sweet glimpses of the family's day-to-day life, offering fans a side of Curry they rarely see during the NBA season.There were no championship celebrations or red-carpet appearances. Instead, the photos reflected warmth, togetherness and gratitude.For a player whose career has unfolded under constant scrutiny, the carousel served as a reminder that some of his most meaningful moments happen far away from the basketball court.While Ayesha gave fans a peek into family life, Stephen Curry recently shared how important his wife became during another unfamiliar chapter of his career. Speaking to people while promoting the animated sports comedy "GOAT," Curry admitted he relied heavily on Ayesha's experience when he stepped into the recording booth. "She's been doing it her whole life. And for the first session we had, she went first, and I got to sit in and watch her work, and it kind of broke the ice a little bit. "Ayesha worked in commercials and entertainment long before Steph's venture into voice acting. According to Curry, watching her in action helped calm his nerves. He also confessed that voice acting proved tougher than expected. "But once you're in there ... I didn't realize how physical it was. It's not like you're just talking, but you gotta really be in that moment."The admission revealed a role reversal within one of sports' most admired marriages. On the court, Stephen Curry often leads by example. In Hollywood, Ayesha guided the way. Her latest Instagram post may not have addressed those comments directly, but the timing felt fitting. The same support system Curry praised publicly showed up again through simple family moments that mattered far more than any spotlight ever could.
Article Summary: Ayesha Curry shared a heartfelt Instagram carousel packed with family memories. The photos reflected warmth, togetherness and gratitude. Curry recently opened up about leaning on his wife during his first major Hollywood experience. The same support system Curry praised publicly showed up again through simple family moments.

Gregg Popovich is not on the San Antonio Spurs bench, but his voice is still reaching his players. With the Spurs facing elimination in Game 5 of the 2026 NBA Finals against the New York Knicks on Saturday night at Frost Bank Center, forward Julian Champagnie revealed that the franchise's legendary coach has been sending texts to keep the team's spirits up and accountability sharp.The message from the winningest head coach in NBA history lands at a critical moment. San Antonio trails 3-1 and needs to win four straight to claim the title, something only the 2016 Cleveland Cavaliers have ever done in the NBA Finals.Champagnie shared the details during an appearance on SiriusXM NBA Radio, describing a coach who has remained deeply invested even from a distance. "I mean, he shoots us all texts to keep our spirits high and stuff like that, tells where we have to be better. As he gets an address as a team or anything like that, he was in film today," Champagnie said."We've seen him today, and I'm sure he'll say stuff to us tomorrow or the next day or whatever it is. But obviously, whenever we see him, we all get that warm feeling, obviously as coach, as coach Pop."Popovich, who is reportedly managing ongoing health concerns and is no longer coaching on the sidelines, has nonetheless maintained an active presence around the organization throughout San Antonio's surprising run to the Finals.His involvement in film sessions and continued communication with players reinforces his status as the spiritual core of this franchise, regardless of what his official capacity may be.The circumstances that produced this elimination game were difficult to watch for Spurs fans. San Antonio built a commanding first-half advantage in Game 4, shooting 14-of-26 from three-point range, the most 3-pointers made in a half in NBA Finals history. But the Knicks turned it around entirely, outscoring the Spurs in the second half behind OG Anunoby's clutch basket and another 30-plus point effort from Jalen Brunson to win 107-106.Victor Wembanyama shot just 21.4% from the field in the second half and missed two crucial free throws in the final two minutes. De'Aaron Fox's late-game miscue compounded the damage. A team that seemed poised to even the series instead handed New York a commanding lead with one of the worst second-half collapses in Finals history.Game 5 tips off Saturday at 8:30 p.m. ET on ABC from Frost Bank Center. San Antonio's crowd will be loud. Whether that, and Pop's texts, are enough to extend this series is the question now sitting in front of a young Spurs team that has already exceeded most expectations.
Article Summary: San Antonio Spurs forward Julian Champagnie says Gregg Popovich has been sending texts to keep the team's spirits up and accountability sharp. San Antonio trails 3-1 and needs to win four straight to claim the title, something only the 2016 Cleveland Cavaliers have ever done in the NBA Finals. Popovich is reportedly managing ongoing health concerns and is no longer coaching on the sidelines.

Article Title: Karl-Anthony Towns' fiancée Jordyn Woods finally reveals the secret behind her lucky purse phenomenon during Knicks' Finals run
Article Text:
Karl-Anthony Towns' fiancée Jordyn Woods. Image via: David Sherman/ NBAE via Getty

How Jordyn Woods' lucky purse became the Knicks' unofficial good luck charm



'The floor was actually vibrating' after OG Anunoby's Game 4 heroics



Karl Towns Sr. never leaves her side at Knicks games



Jordyn Woods did not set out to become a talisman. But somewhere between a signature orange bag and a Madison Square Garden crowd shaking so hard the floor vibrated, that is exactly what happened.Woods, the fashion designer and fiancée of New York Knicks center Karl-Anthony Towns, has become one of the defining sideline figures of the 2026 NBA Finals. Ahead of Game 5 against the San Antonio Spurs, she opened up about her game-day rituals, her famous lucky purse, and what it has felt like to watch New York's basketball faithful transform an entire city.The orange bag was never supposed to mean anything. Woods was clear about that. "It happened very organically. It wasn't intentional. I was never the one that was like, 'This is the lucky bag. Everyone needs to say that,'" she told The Hollywood Reporter . "I didn't create the phenomenon, social media did, but I'm definitely loving it."The bag, from her own "Woods by Jordyn" label, gained traction as fans noticed a pattern: when Woods brought it to games, the Knicks won. When she couldn't, they didn't. The pressure mounted quickly. "It is immense pressure," she admitted, "that the one time I couldn't bring the bag, and we lost."When a no-bag policy went into effect at MSG during Game 3 of the Finals, Woods improvised without hesitation. She swapped the purse for a pair of matching tangerine orange ostrich shoes, keeping the color, if not the accessory.New York won Game 4 anyway.Game 4 at Madison Square Garden produced one of the more visceral crowd moments Woods has experienced since Towns joined the Knicks. After OG Anunoby's game-sealing basket sent the building into chaos, the sensation was physical. "The ceiling after OG put the shot back, the floor was actually vibrating," she said. "You could feel the room was shaking; it was so crazy."That moment captured something Woods has been processing throughout this entire postseason run: the singular intensity of New York's basketball fandom. "The city is unlike anything I've ever seen. I've never seen a fandom like this, and no matter who the person is, they're fully there, cheering on their team. No shame. No one's trying to be too cool or too anything."She said the effects extend well beyond the arena. "Crime's down in the city, people are coming together, the economy is booming, people's small businesses are doing better. There's a lot of positivity coming out of the Knicks winning."Through every home and away game in the Finals, Woods has had one constant companion in the stands. Karl Towns Sr., Towns' father, sits beside her at every game, including Saturday's Game 5 in San Antonio. It is a detail that matters to her. Towns Sr. has been a steady, grounding presence throughout what has become an emotionally charged run.As for Towns himself, she said his reaction to the whole lucky-purse saga has been entirely on brand. "He's here for it. He's always been my biggest support, my No. 1 support. So he's loving that both of us get to win in the process."Woods described her game-day preparation with the same specificity she brings to the bag. She does her own makeup, films a get-ready-with-me before deciding whether to post it, packs Reese's x Oreos as an emergency snack, and selects her outfit the night before or sometimes two hours out. Road games call for more casual looks, she noted, because navigating an unfamiliar arena adds enough unpredictability already.Heading into a potential closeout game, she is keeping expectations managed and emotions in check. "I feel the same. I'm excited; I'm nervous; I'm ready to just send all the positive vibes and energy." As for any championship plans, the couple has made none. "We have not planned anything, we're like 'Let's just get through each day as it comes.'" She did add one detail: "I am in my bridal era, so we have a lot to look forward to. "The orange shoes are packed. What happens next is up to the Knicks.
Article Summary: Jordyn Woods' lucky purse has become the Knicks' unofficial good luck charm. The bag, from her own "Woods by Jordyn" label, gained traction as fans noticed a pattern: when Woods brought it to games, the Knicks won. Karl Towns Sr. never leaves her side at Knicks games.
